req.title: Create patient
req.statement: A registered clinician shall create a patient resource with validated demographics; creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-001 mandates that a clinician can create a Patient resource with valid demographics (positive path) and that creation is rejected with HTTP 422 when mandatory elements are absent (negative/error path). Three heuristics are applied: EP covers the happy-path partition where all mandatory fields are present; NEG covers the missing-mandatory-element partition; BVA probes the exact boundary of a minimally-complete payload (exactly the set of required fields, no extras). Each test is self-contained via a clinician-scoped auth fixture and cleans up any created resource in a finalizer to remain idempotent.

tests.name: test_create_patient_all_mandatory_fields_returns_201
preconditions: Caller holds a valid clinician JWT (role:clinician)
Target service fhir-lite is reachable at BASE_URL
No prior patient with the test identifier exists
actions: Build a FHIR Patient payload containing resourceType, name (family+given), gender, birthDate, and identifier
POST payload to /Patient with Authorization: Bearer <clinician_token>
expected: Response body contains a server-assigned id
Stored patient demographics match submitted values
trace: R-HLT-001 -> 'registered clinician shall create a patient resource with validated demographics'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_all_mandatory_fields_returns_201(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, cleanup_patient):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": "EP-001"}],
        "name": [{"family": "Smith", "given": ["Alice"]}],
        "gender": "female",
        "birthDate": "1990-06-15"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Patient"
    assert "id" in body
    assert body["name"][0]["family"] == "Smith"
    assert body["gender"] == "female"
    cleanup_patient(body["id"])

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_family_name_returns_422
preconditions: Caller holds a valid clinician JWT (role:clinician)
Target service fhir-lite is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a Patient payload intentionally omitting the mandatory 'name.family' element
POST the incomplete payload to /Patient with Authorization: Bearer <clinician_token>
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error detail referencing the missing field
trace: R-HLT-001 -> 'creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected'
R-HLT-001 -> 'Error pathways: 422 when mandatory element missing'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_missing_family_name_returns_422(clinician_auth_headers, base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": "NEG-001"}],
        "name": [{"given": ["NoFamily"]}],
        "gender": "male",
        "birthDate": "1985-03-22"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body)
    assert any(kw in error_text.lower() for kw in ["family", "name", "mandatory", "required", "missing"]), \
        f"Error body did not reference missing field: {body}"

tests.name: test_create_patient_minimal_valid_payload_exactly_required_fields
preconditions: Caller holds a valid clinician JWT (role:clinician)
Target service fhir-lite is reachable at BASE_URL
Payload contains exactly the minimum set of mandatory fields and nothing else
actions: Build a Patient payload with only the minimum required fields: resourceType, name.family, gender, birthDate
Omit all optional fields (identifier, telecom, address, etc.)
POST the minimal payload to /Patient with Authorization: Bearer <clinician_token>
expected: HTTP 201 Created (boundary: exactly-sufficient mandatory data is accepted)
Response body contains a server-assigned id
No optional fields injected by server cause assertion failures
trace: R-HLT-001 -> 'validated demographics' boundary: minimum viable demographic set
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_minimal_valid_payload_exactly_required_fields(clinician_auth_headers, base_url, cleanup_patient):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "name": [{"family": "Doe"}],
        "gender": "unknown",
        "birthDate": "2000-01-01"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 for minimal payload, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Patient"
    assert "id" in body, "Server must assign an id to the created resource"
    cleanup_patient(body["id"])

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_birthdate_returns_422
preconditions: Caller holds a valid clinician JWT (role:clinician)
Target service fhir-lite is reachable at BASE_URL
Decision table row: name=present, gender=present, birthDate=ABSENT -> reject
actions: Build a Patient payload with name.family and gender present but birthDate omitted
POST payload to /Patient with Authorization: Bearer <clinician_token>
expected: Response body references birthDate or mandatory field validation failure
trace: R-HLT-001 -> 'creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected'
R-HLT-001 -> 'Error pathways: 422 when mandatory element missing'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_missing_birthdate_returns_422(clinician_auth_headers, base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "name": [{"family": "Jones", "given": ["Bob"]}],
        "gender": "male"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body)
    assert any(kw in error_text.lower() for kw in ["birthdate", "birth", "mandatory", "required", "missing"]), \
        f"Error body did not reference missing birthDate: {body}"
